---
pagetitle: "Decision-support and benefit-cost tools | Research | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Turning preference and economic evidence into interactive decision-support and benefit-cost tools that decision-makers can use directly."
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="Breadcrumb"><a href="/">Home</a> / <a href="/research/">Research</a> / <span aria-current="page">Decision-support and benefit-cost tools</span></nav>
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Research programme</p><h1>Decision-support and benefit-cost tools</h1></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow">
<h2>Overview</h2>
<p>A study ends with a paper, but a decision needs something more usable. This strand of work turns preference estimates and economic evidence into interactive tools that non-specialists can use to explore options.</p>
<h2>Approach</h2>
<p>Each tool is transparent about which figures are estimates and which are assumptions, and is designed to be usable by the person who needs it, not only by its author.</p>
<h2>Tools</h2>
<ul class="cv-list">
<li><a href="/tools/emandeval/">eMANDEVAL Future</a> for vaccine-mandate policy.</li>
<li><a href="/tools/soil-crc-bca/">SOIL CRC Benefit-Cost Analysis Tool</a> for agricultural trials.</li>
<li><a href="/tools/steps/">STEPS FETP Scale-up Decision Aid</a> for field-epidemiology training.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Related resources</h2>
<ul class="cv-list"><li><a href="/tools/">All tools</a></li><li><a href="/blog/posts/why-i-build-decision-tools/">Why I build decision tools</a></li></ul>
</div></section>
```
